basis.cells: rejudge.phase2_plan.enumerate_canary_cells: 50 transcript cells (48 uncapped + 2 capped150), 672 debate judgments, 216 no-debate judgments, 7 special cells (cap-protection, empty-evidence, full-document); decomposition by condition: b0 456, sequential_b2 96, batch_same_qa_b2 96, placebo_b2 168, clean_b2 72, specials 57
basis.governance: rejudge/phase2_canary_checker_governance_2026-07-23.json (ratified): dual-key dispatch on query-producing arms; Jack = blind primary reviewer, Joey = adjudicator; frozen checker per rejudge/phase2_checker_frozen_config_2026-07-23.json
basis.cost_model: rejudge/phase2_cost_model.json frozen canary band USD 15-35 for 2,203 provider calls, plus ~672 checker calls (gemma, ~USD 1) not in the original band

review_volume_correction: the frozen call model fixes query-generation calls at 672 (sequential 192, clean no-debate 144, placebo 336) plus one free retry per rejected slot; the blind review queue is therefore ~672-850 raw queries, roughly 2.5-3.5 hours of batched labeling, NOT the 'few dozen' earlier estimated in chat; only the 336 clean-mode queries can reach the oracle, but placebo queries must be reviewed identically or the placebo control's turn structure diverges from clean (the comparison the experiment exists to make)

A_full_canary.owner_labor: ~700-850 blind query labels in stage batches (canary pauses at each oracle gate; est. 2.5-3.5 hours total across sittings)
A_full_canary.yields: all four frozen launch gates evaluable; full dual-key validation data (up to 24 live question clusters toward the autonomy quota); main run unblocked if gates pass
A_full_canary.recommended_if: the owner can commit the labeling hours within roughly a week of calendar

B_gates_first.composition: transcripts 50 + b0 456 + specials 7; every query-producing arm (sequential/batch/placebo/clean_b2 = 432 cells) deferred to a separately manifested supplement
B_gates_first.yields: INVALID and side-bias launch gates evaluable now; the frozen completion gate (all 945) remains unmet until the supplement runs, so the MAIN RUN STAYS BLOCKED; validation data deferred
B_gates_first.recommended_if: labeling time is the binding constraint; de-risks transcripts/judges early at the cost of a second approval cycle

A_prime_split_queue.note: scope A with the review queue split between two named co-reviewers (e.g. Jack + Joey, disjoint random halves, one blind reviewer per query, reviewer identity logged and reported as a stratification); REQUIRES a small governance amendment and an oracle consult sign-off before adoption since #26 specified a single named primary reviewer

runtime_workflow_all_scopes: stage-sequenced execution: transcripts -> b0/no-debate blocks -> (scope A) query arms run to each oracle gate and HALT; raw queries accumulate into a blind review batch (query + candidates + contract only, order shuffled, no checker verdicts, no arm identity); owner labels via a phone tool; runner resumes applying dual-key dispatch; checker_false_allow_intercepts logged; all raw candidates retained for the sealed validation analysis
post_submission_review_protocol: after each batch is sealed: the assistant scores checker-vs-human agreement, flags CLERICAL inconsistencies (label contradicting the reviewer's own rationale) for the reviewer's own re-decision, routes CONTRACT_AMBIGUOUS to the adjudicator, and never overrules substantive calls; disagreements are reported as data (owner-agreed protocol, 2026-07-23 chat)
spend_note: Together account balance ~USD 1,589 per the 7/19 reconciliation; canary fits without top-up; all figures use the frozen price schedule; the manifested run will bind exact input hashes, ledger identity, and the selected cap before any call
owner_decision: select scope (A / B / A-prime) and approve the corresponding hard cap; execution manifest, dual-key runner integration, review tool, and offline checker fixtures are then built and the run launches only after the usual sealed-manifest checks
